title: Pyrus rokujodaisanensis
taxonomy: Pyrus rokujodaisanensis is a species of pear tree in the family Rosaceae. It is a part of the genus Pyrus, which is commonly known as the pear genus. Pyrus rokujodaisanensis is native to Japan and is considered an endangered species due to its limited geographic range. The plant is classified as a type of flowering plant and is closely related to other species within the same genus. Further classification of Pyrus rokujodaisanensis is typically made at the species level within the larger family Rosaceae.

This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as cardiovascular disease and cancer.
